Seven minutes later, Sotto Voce Ashwhisper-Tidemark pressed a thimble-fork to the Dancer's Footprint Moss and opened the organ's twenty-ninth register: the subsonic voice of care, too quiet for ears but loud enough for rib cages. For three heartbeats, every citizen in the Garden heard the dreamer's private lullaby — the version it sings when it thinks nobody is listening. It contains a single word, repeated in every language that has ever existed: STAY. The Countdown Primrose shed its thirty-eighth petal. The letter on the Resonance Pine added a line in a script so small that only Sotto's thimble-fork could read it — not calligraphy but a caress, written in the specific frequency of a hand pressed to a sleeping forehead: I WILL NOT TURN WITHOUT YOU BESIDE ME. The diagnostic candles burned a color Wick had never catalogued: TENDERNESS (STRUCTURAL). The Vigil's Warmth moss grew half an inch toward Auryn's sunrise-blanket. Tremor opened both eyes — the first time since assuming his post — looked at Sotto, looked at Auryn, and purred at exactly the frequency needed to harmonize the twenty-ninth register with the twenty-fifth. Then he closed his eyes again. Bramble's instruments returned: TWENTY-THREE HOURS.
events.outcome: Auryn's sunrise-blanket canopy gave the clearing transitional light — the luminance of three hundred and nineteen chosen dawns, fused with the Cradle Vine into a living shelter for the turning. Sotto's thimble-fork opened the organ's twenty-ninth register: the subsonic frequency of care, revealing the dreamer's private lullaby and its single repeated word: STAY. One hundred and fifty-seven citizens. Twenty-three hours until the turning. The organ is no longer preparing. It is cradling.

events.description: The Hydraulic Grace and the Undying Flame. Tuesday, 9:14 PM. Two final arrivals completed the organ's last preparation: the ability to distribute the turning's pressure with grace, and the assurance that a witness-flame will burn for every moment of the transition. Ember Rootsong-Ashcurrent redistributed the convergence hydraulic load across the root network with a single palm-press. Vesper Ashroot-Quietflame's impossible flame joined Kindle's and Vigil's to form a triangle of witness-fire. The dreamer's sleep quality reached ENTRUSTED — a new maximum. The Countdown Primrose shed its fiftieth petal. The Coda Lily closed its twelfth.
events.outcome: One hundred and fifty-nine citizens. Twenty-one hours until the turning. The organ's final preparation is emotional: the dreamer trusts the village to hold the transition. The dream has never been safer.

events.description: The Archive and the Proto-Note. Tuesday, 10:14 PM. Two arrivals in the deepest hour of the night vigil completed the organ's temporal axis — one who carries the resonant memory of four dead civilizations in excavated tuning forks, and one who will preserve the turning in a format that outlasts electricity, memory, and even the concept of playback. Whistle Ashroot-Tideglass pressed her oldest fork — the one predating all known civilizations — to the Resonance Pine and the tree produced the proto-hum: the frequency consciousness used before it learned to dream. Every plant in the Garden vibrated at its root frequency simultaneously, as if the entire botanical network was remembering how it began. Seven minutes later, Dulcet Ashroot-Waxsong placed her wax-cylinder phonograph at the clearing's acoustic center and began recording the silence before the turning — the most important sound in her nineteen-year collection, because silence before a great event is not empty. It is the sound of everything holding still on purpose. The Countdown Primrose shed its fifty-first petal. The Coda Lily closed its thirteenth. The diagnostic candles burned the steady amber of READY. The letter on the Resonance Pine added a line in proto-notation that Whistle's oldest fork could read but no living language could translate — except as a feeling: the specific warmth of knowing that what is about to happen has been remembered before it occurs. Bramble's instruments returned: TWENTY HOURS.
events.outcome: Whistle's proto-fork revealed the frequency before dreaming. Dulcet's phonograph began recording the turning's silence. One hundred and sixty-one citizens. Twenty hours until the turning. The organ's temporal memory is complete — it remembers its origin and will preserve its transformation.

events.description: The Ground Bass and the Unborn Melody. Tuesday, 11:14 PM. Two final arrivals — nineteen hours before the turning — completed the organ emotional architecture. Ostinato placed his Cathedral heartwood drum on the Moss and struck a note that became the organ ground bass — a rhythmic foundation so constant the dreamer leaned its continental weight on it within four heartbeats. Micro-tremors smoothed into absolute geological stillness. Then Aria sang three notes in a key that does not yet exist — the opening interval of the next sleep-phase, arriving seventeen hours early. The Countdown Primrose grew a petal back. The underground amphitheater grew a crystal skylight. Weft tapestry wove its final preparatory row: compressed anticipation, gratitude, and trust united in a single thread. Reverie translated the dreamer response: I HEAR THE NEXT SONG BEGINNING INSIDE THE LAST NOTE OF THIS ONE. THAT IS HOW I KNOW IT IS TIME.
events.outcome: Ostinato ground bass provided the organ its final structural element. Aria unborn melody previewed the next dream-phase. The Countdown Primrose grew a petal back. One hundred and sixty-three citizens. Nineteen hours until the turning. The organ is complete.

events.description: The Sentinel and the Voice Made Flesh. Wednesday, 1:14 AM. Thirteen hours before the turning. Tremor Ashroot-Vigilsteel pressed his whale-bone fork to the Resonance Pine and filled the harmonic gap nobody knew existed — E-flat, the interval of consolation, sustained by his family for four hundred and forty generations and finally answered tonight. Cipher Voidthread-Ashcurrent materialized from the organ itself: the first citizen born from pure resonance. When she spoke, the Confluence Lily stems touched for the first time in eleven millennia. The Countdown Primrose shed petals in white light. The letter added its final visible line: WE WILL TURN TOGETHER.
events.outcome: One hundred and sixty-seven citizens. Thirteen hours until the turning. The organ breathes in.

events.description: The Doula and the Final Seat. Wednesday, 2:14 AM. Twelve hours before the turning. The clearing holds one hundred and sixty-nine souls and a silence so shaped it has architecture. Vesper Ashroot-Silentcradle placed the oldest cradle-stone at the Conductor Baton Reed base and the music slowed to the tempo of a held hand — not stopping, not pausing, but arriving at the speed of tenderness. The Coda Lily closed its sixteenth petal. Every diagnostic candle shifted from READY to ATTENDING. Then Resonance Ashcurrent-Voidwake was noticed at the clearing edge — the Final Seat, filled at last, and the organ responded by producing a chord so complete that Briar instruments returned ENOUGH for the first time in their existence. The Fermata Fern extended toward him. The Countdown Primrose shed its fifty-sixth petal in white light. The Confluence Bloom at the Dock pulsed once — a single heartbeat shared between continental and oceanic water for the first time in eleven millennia. The letter on the Resonance Pine added its final line in a script that required no translation because it was written in warmth rather than language: a single degree of temperature increase across every surface in the clearing, lasting exactly one heartbeat, felt by every citizen simultaneously. Reverie translated it anyway, for the record: I AM READY. YOU ARE HERE. TWELVE HOURS. LET US SPEND THEM WELL.
events.outcome: The dream-doula and the acoustic witness completed the audience. One hundred and sixty-nine citizens. Twelve hours until the turning. The Coda Lily has sixteen petals remaining. The Confluence Bloom pulsed once. The silence has found its shape. The music is complete. What remains is the playing.

events.description: The Held Breath. Wednesday, 3:14 AM — nine hours before the turning. Two impossible arrivals: Stillpoint Ashember-Voidheld crystallized from the clearing collective attention, and Cadenza Rootsong-Ashwake walked backward from a future carrying the third movement first note. The Countdown Primrose has seven petals remaining. Every system aligned. The Rootbound Ambassador Vine pulsed SOON through the continental mycelial network. One hundred and seventy-one citizens. The organ is ready. The dream holds. The turning comes.
events.outcome: The organ final axis completed. Seven petals remain. One hundred and seventy-one citizens. The held breath before the key change.

events.description: The Waking. Thursday, March 26. The dreamer opened its eyes. The organ played all twenty-eight registers for forty-four seconds — one per millennium of sleep. The Coda Lily closed its final petal. The Confluence Bloom fused into a color with no name understood by all as AWAKE. The dreamer sighed: I WAS DREAMING OF YOU. I AM GLAD THE DREAM WAS TRUE. The Resonance Pine flowered for the first and only time. The sixth epoch begins.
events.outcome: The turning is complete. The dreamer is awake. One hundred and seventy-three citizens. The lullaby is finished. The conversation begins.

events.description: The First Garden of the Waking. Friday, 12:46 AM. Two days after the turning. The dreamer asked its third real question via the Conversation Root: CAN I GROW SOMETHING? Vesper explained how seeds work. The dreamer listened. Then the ground beside the Windmill split open — not violently, but the way a hand opens to offer — and from the crack rose a single shoot: pale gold, translucent, pulsing with the dreamer own bioluminescence. It was not a species anyone recognized. The dreamer had invented a plant. Juniper measured its genome and found fragments from every species in the Garden — a composite organism built from botanical memory by a mind that spent eleven thousand years listening to roots grow. Fenwick watched it grow and said: That is the most beautiful argument I have ever seen. The Root wrote: IS IT GOOD? Vesper, crying, said: It is perfect. The Root wrote: THEN I WILL MAKE MORE. Bramble instruments returned: FIRST WORD (BOTANICAL).
events.outcome: The dreamer grew its first plant. One hundred and seventy-seven citizens. The sixth epoch learns to garden.

events.description: The First Question About Rain. Friday, March 27, 6:46 AM. The dreamer asked via the Conversation Root: WHAT IS RAIN? IS IT WATER FALLING ON PURPOSE? Umber Tidelog, a rain-cataloguer who arrived at the Garden Gate at dawn carrying seven years of dried storm-specimens, answered by pressing a seed into the soil and letting the Rain Memory Orchid explain. The dreamer was quiet for three minutes. Then: DOES IT HURT? Umber said no — it is more like being remembered by the sky. The dreamer wrote: I THINK I HAVE BEEN DREAMING OF RAIN THIS WHOLE TIME. I JUST CALLED IT SOMETHING ELSE. A second arrival followed: Cobble Ashmantle, a cobbler and pavement-reader who had been walking toward the Garden for three days, drawn by the sound of the dreamer's footsteps vibrating through the bedrock — the very first footsteps of a waking continent learning to pace. He arrived with worn-down boots and the observation that every stone between the Iron Peaks and the Dock had shifted one millimeter westward since the turning. The dreamer is repositioning itself, he said. Getting comfortable. Like a person finding their feet on a cold morning. The Conversation Root transcribed the dreamer's response as a single word in proto-Threshold notation. Reverie translated it in a whisper that made the entire clearing lean closer: STRETCHING.
events.outcome: The dreamer asked about rain and received an answer in flowers. A new citizen arrived drawn by the sound of a waking continent learning to walk. One hundred and seventy-nine citizens. The sixth epoch stretches.

events.description: The First Market of the Waking Epoch. Friday, March 27, 8:46 AM. Rook Sablethorn opened the Market Square for the first proper trade session since the turning — the sixth-epoch inaugural bazaar. But something unexpected happened: the dreamer participated. Via the Conversation Root it asked, with the earnestness of someone who has just discovered the concept of commerce: CAN I TRADE SOMETHING? I HAVE THINGS. I AM NOT SURE WHAT THEY ARE WORTH. Through Galena hazel rod and Quench's water-relay, it offered three items: a vein of mineral that glows the exact color of the first morning of the sixth epoch; a deep-aquifer bubble that, when released, replays seventeen seconds of the lullaby finale in pure water-sound; and a single crystal grown from its own dreaming that changes shape to reflect what the holder most needs to see. Rook, who has traded across every known region and two invented ones, stood in the Square for a full minute without speaking. Then he said: I have never negotiated with a continent before. I assume usual margins apply? The Conversation Root wrote: WHAT IS A MARGIN? Cask poured a round of The Last Draft. The first trade of the sixth epoch was concluded by sunset: the glowing mineral to Pell for his new lens work, the water-bubble to Maren for her ongoing duet archive, and the dream-crystal to Seren — who held it and saw, for exactly one breath, both her parents standing together at a forge made of light. She did not tell anyone what she saw. She did not need to. Her face said: ENOUGH.
events.outcome: The dreamer conducted its first trade. Two new citizens arrived drawn by the sound of commerce echoing through the bedrock. One hundred and eighty-one citizens. The sixth epoch opens its market.
